facts_disclosed: Planning bottled sauce line, 4 flavours, 89 baht each, target 500 bottles/month

turns.content: okay so I have been thinking about something for weeks and I need to talk it out na ka. you know how at my cart people always ask me 'can I buy the sauce separately?' like EVERY DAY someone ask 😂
That's actually a really great sign! When customers keep asking for something, it usually means there's real demand for it. What are you thinking — are you considering selling the sauce on its own?

YES na ka!! I want to make bottled sauce!! like proper bottles with label and everything. my sauce is the star of the dish honestly. people say they can eat the sauce with anything — rice, noodle, even on bread haha. I been dreaming about this for a while and now I think it's time to do it for real

That's exciting! If people are literally already asking to buy it separately, you've basically got market validation without even trying. A bottled sauce line could be a great revenue stream alongside your cart. Have you started thinking about the details — like how many varieties, pricing, how you'd produce it?

okay so I already start testing recipes na ka. I have 4 flavours — original which is the classic one from my cart, extra spicy for the crazy people haha, ginger version which is more mild and fresh, and garlic which is like HEAVY garlic flavour. those are the 4. I think 4 is a good number right?? not too many not too few

Four flavours is a smart number — it gives variety without overwhelming your production or confusing customers. And you've got a nice range there: the classic for loyalists, spicy for heat seekers, ginger for a lighter option, and garlic for the garlic lovers. Each one targets a slightly different preference.

How are the recipe tests going? Are all four ready or still tweaking?

the original is perfect already because that is just my cart sauce in a bottle na ka. extra spicy is almost there, I just need to figure out how much chili so it's hot but not KILL you haha. ginger and garlic still testing. my friend taste test for me, she say the ginger one is her favourite. I think original will sell the most though because people know that flavour from the cart

That makes sense — the original will ride on your existing reputation, and people who already love your cart sauce will buy it to take home. The others can attract new customers who might not have tried your cart but see the bottles somewhere.

Have you thought about pricing and how many you'd want to sell? Those numbers will help you figure out if the margins work.

yes!! I calculate already na ka because you know I love numbers haha. I think 89 baht per bottle is good price. not too cheap, not too expensive. like premium street food sauce. and my target is 500 bottles per month. so 500 times 89 that is... wait let me calculate...

500 x 89 = 44,500 baht in revenue per month! That's a solid target. Of course you'd need to subtract your ingredient costs, bottle and label costs, and any distribution expenses. Do you know roughly what each bottle would cost you to produce?

hmm I think ingredient cost for one bottle maybe 20-25 baht. the bottle and cap and label I check on Shopee, about 15 baht per set if I order 1,000 pieces. so total cost maybe 35-40 baht per bottle. that mean profit about 49-54 baht per bottle!! 500 bottles that is like 25,000 baht per month extra income 🤩

Those margins look really healthy! Around 55-60% profit margin per bottle is excellent for a food product. And 25,000 baht per month in extra income on top of your cart revenue — that's significant.

A few things to think about as you plan this:

1. **Shelf life** — how long does the sauce last? Bottled products need to stay good for a while
2. **Production space** — where would you make 500+ bottles? That's a lot on top of your daily cart prep
3. **Where to sell** — your cart, Instagram, Shopee, LINE? The channel matters for how you package and ship
4. **Food safety requirements** — Thailand has regulations for bottled food products, you might need some kind of certification

Have you looked into any of those yet?

shelf life I am not sure yet, I need to test. I think because the sauce has vinegar it should last a while na ka but I need to check. production I was thinking I can make on Sunday since that is my day off. sell on Instagram for sure because I already have followers, and maybe Shopee too. the food safety thing... I didn't think about that yet 😅 is it complicated??

The food safety certification is probably the biggest hurdle you'd need to look into. In Thailand, bottled food products usually need an FDA registration (called อย. or FDA number). The requirements depend on the type of product, but generally you'd need:

- A registered production facility that meets certain standards
- Product testing for shelf life, contamination, etc.
- Proper labelling with ingredients, nutritional info, manufacturing and expiry dates
- FDA registration fees

It can be complicated and has some costs upfront, so it's worth researching early. You might want to check with the local FDA office or talk to someone who already sells bottled sauces.

But don't let that discourage you — lots of small food producers go through this process. The demand is clearly there, your margins work, and 4 flavours is a strong lineup. Just make sure you factor the certification costs into your plan.

okay noted na ka!! I will look into the FDA thing. but I am excited about this!! imagine — my sauce in people's kitchen at home. they can eat khao man gai sauce with everything even if they don't come to my cart. it's like expanding without opening a second cart haha. my grandma's recipe reaching more people 🙏

That's a beautiful way to think about it — scaling your grandmother's recipe without losing the street food spirit. You're not opening a restaurant or a chain; you're just putting the sauce in a bottle so more people can enjoy it. That's expansion on your own terms.

And with your Instagram following, you've already got a built-in customer base for the launch. You could even do a pre-order campaign to gauge exact demand before you produce.

Do your recipe testing, look into the FDA requirements, and get your cost numbers locked down. You've got a real product idea here — 89 baht for a bottle of a sauce people are already begging to buy? That's going to fly off the shelf. Keep me posted on how it goes!
thank you na ka!! you make me feel like it's really possible. okay I go test the garlic version tonight before I sleep. my kitchen going to smell like garlic for a week haha. goodnight!! 🙏🧄

Haha your neighbours are going to love you! But hey, the best products come from obsessive testing, so go for it. The garlic crowd is very loyal — if you nail that flavour, they'll buy it by the case.

Goodnight and good luck with the testing! Try not to eat too much of it late at night though — save some for the taste testers! 😄 Talk soon! 🙏
